facts: It would take 8.5 minutes to boil in space's vacuum at 200°F
Space is 100 times more empty than the emptiest football field
A human body would expand 2.5 times its size in space's zero pressure
idea.payoff: and we still don't know how our bodies would react long-term
idea.title: Boil in Space? 🚀
idea.description: Imagine being in the vastness of space, where the emptiness is so extreme it's like nothing on Earth 🌌. If you were to be exposed to space without protection, your body would undergo some astonishing and terrifying changes 🤯. Will humans ever be able to survive the harsh conditions of space?

scenes.visual: Dark, star-filled space. A stylized astronaut helmet appears, with subtle steam or bubbles around it.
A digital timer graphic counting down/up, overlaid on a distant starfield. A stylized human silhouette briefly appears next to it, with a subtle 'boiling' effect.
An animation showing a green football field outline quickly shrinking into a tiny speck, then disappearing into a massive, empty cosmic void with sparse stars.
A minimalist, abstract human outline inflates and expands to 2.5 times its original size, like a balloon. Background is dark, empty space.
A solitary astronaut figure looks out into a vast, beautiful but mysterious nebula. A subtle, shimmering question mark appears in the background.
